title: Greek Salad
tagline: Horiatiki - the real deal, no lettuce required.
description: Authentic Greek village salad with chunky tomatoes, cucumbers, red onion, olives, and a slab of feta. Dressed simply with olive oil and oregano.
ingredients: 1 English cucumber, halved and sliced thick
steps: Cut tomatoes into wedges, cucumbers into half-moons, peppers into rings, and onion into thin slices.
Arrange vegetables on a platter or in a wide shallow bowl. Scatter olives over top.
Place the block of feta directly on top. Don't crumble it - that's not traditional.
Drizzle generously with olive oil and a splash of red wine vinegar.
Sprinkle oregano, salt, and pepper over everything.
Serve immediately with crusty bread to soak up the juices.
notes: Traditional Greek salad has no lettuce. Just chunky vegetables.
Use the best olive oil you have - it's doing a lot of work here.
The feta should be a whole slab, not crumbled. Each person breaks off what they want.
